text: Mom. Mom.
>> What? What?
>> Dahi just WhatsAppapped me asking if
we're hanging out for New Year's Eve.
>> What? Where did she get that idea? Bring
me my phone. Let me WhatsApp them all.
New Year's messages before they get
ideas that we're hanging out. Bring me
my phone. Go bring my phone.
>> Your phone is right there.
>> Oh, thank you for putting it here. It's
New Year's and my husband and I don't
communicate anyways. So, I'm not writing
a paragraph. I'm sending a sticker. But
WhatsApp's New Year's stickers
collection is making low effort seem
more fun and special, which thank God
because he's not getting anything else.
On New Year's Eve, over 100 billion
messages get sent on WhatsApp and each
one stays private. Even WhatsApp doesn't
see them. My favorite New Year's feature
is when you react with the celebration
emoji, and confetti flies everywhere, as
if the app is proud of you. The bar is
low, but I still appreciate it. Catching
up has never been faster or easier than
on WhatsApp. And yes, even with my
mother-in-law, if she calls me one more
time, she's getting blocked. So many
people to wish. Thank God for WhatsApp.
This could have gone on and on. Oh my
god. Oh no, we're not going out. I
already wished you a beautiful happy new
year with the stickers and everything.
My New Year's resolution, I'm not
leaving the house until I want to. Happy
New Year.
